Name: fundamental
Meaning: Ontic, generator, handoff, invariant, or root-contract level. Failure changes the meaning of the derived-only architecture or a pillar boundary.
Documentation requirement: Full dossier required before promotion or consumption: definition, natural provenance, exact source objects, proof target, proof sketch, verification/audit, consumer rights, and semantic uncertainty.
Promotion / demotion rule: May not be promoted by generated prose or status color. Must cite checked Lean code or an explicit governance contract and must remain expert-reviewable.

Name: core
Meaning: Core derived object, API seam, comparison gate, or proof route that many later phases depend on, but not itself an ontic root.
Documentation requirement: Extended dossier: definition, source chain, main lemmas/proof obligations, dependency risks, and downstream consumers. Enough detail for later implementation without re-reading chat history.
Promotion / demotion rule: May be promoted to F0 only if it becomes a pillar-boundary or generator-level contract; may be demoted only after no downstream dependency relies on it.

Name: standard
Meaning: Ordinary derived object, target object, or reference/comparison profile with local but real mathematical or planning relevance.
Documentation requirement: Standard dossier: short definition, source objects, proof target, implementation location, and next documentation action. No large narrative unless it blocks implementation.
Promotion / demotion rule: Promote to F1 if several later phases consume it, if it crosses a handoff/API boundary, or if an expert-review risk is discovered.

Name: auxiliary
Meaning: Support, migration, tooling, generated artifact, ledger, or narrow refactor helper. Important for auditability but not a mathematical generator.
Documentation requirement: Compact record: what exists, why it exists, owning phase, source location, and whether it may affect Lean objects. Avoid proofbook bloat.
Promotion / demotion rule: Promote to F2/F1 only if it starts carrying semantic proof obligations or becomes a required release gate.

Name: mention-only
Meaning: Trivial, navigational, future-side, purely illustrative, or low-risk reference row. It should be visible but not exhaustively documented.
Documentation requirement: Mention and pointer only: name, context class, status, and reason for non-consumption. No full proof dossier unless promoted.
Promotion / demotion rule: Automatically promote if it becomes a dependency, is consumed by Lean code, controls access/release, or carries a reference-theory risk.
